name: Doctor Who
description: The long-running BBC sci-fi series about a time-traveling alien with a blue box.

questions.q: What is the name of the Doctor's time machine, which appears as a 1960s British police box?

source.notes: Time And Relative Dimension In Space.

questions.q: What alien species is the Doctor?

source.notes: From the planet Gallifrey.

questions.q: Doctor Who originally debuted on BBC in what year?

source.notes: 23 November 1963, the day after Kennedy's assassination.

questions.q: Which recurring enemies chant 'EXTERMINATE!' and are known for their ray-gun-like protrusions?

source.notes: Created by Terry Nation, first appeared 1963.

questions.q: Which terrifying monsters appear only as stone statues when you look at them?

source.notes: Introduced in 'Blink' (2007), written by Steven Moffat.

questions.q: Which actor played the Tenth Doctor, coining the catchphrase 'Allons-y!'?

source.notes: Tennant served 2005-2010 and again briefly in 2023.

questions.q: The Doctor is often accompanied by travel partners known as what?

source.notes: A revolving cast of Companions spans the series.

questions.q: Which actor played the Eleventh Doctor, frequently seen in a bowtie and tweed?

source.notes: Smith served 2010-2013.

questions.q: Which Scottish actor took over as the Twelfth Doctor in 2014?

source.notes: Capaldi had previously appeared as a different character in 'The Fires of Pompeii.'

questions.q: Who became the first woman to officially take on the title role as the Thirteenth Doctor?

source.notes: Whittaker served 2017-2022.

questions.q: Which Doctor re-launched the modern series in 2005?

source.notes: The Ninth Doctor, leather-jacketed and northern.

questions.q: What is the name of Rose Tyler's mother and father, her companions across two realities?

source.notes: Played by Camille Coduri and Shaun Dingwall.

questions.q: Which British actor plays the fez-loving, charismatic River Song in New Who?

source.notes: River's identity is the daughter of Amy and Rory, revealed in Series 6.

questions.q: Which redhead Scottish companion first travels with the Eleventh Doctor?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: fixed original_a metadata (was 'Jessica'). Played by Karen Gillan.

questions.q: What is the Doctor's 'go-to' alias when pretending to be human?

source.notes: Used since the Second Doctor era.

questions.q: Which John Hurt character is introduced in the 2013 50th-anniversary special, 'The Day of the Doctor'?

source.notes: The War Doctor fought in the Time War.

questions.q: What iconic device does the Doctor use to manipulate circuits, locks, and sensors?

source.notes: Introduced in 1968, revived in 2005.

questions.q: Which villain, an evil Time Lord, has long been the Doctor's archnemesis?

source.notes: Recent incarnations include Missy (Michelle Gomez) and the Sacha Dhawan Master.

questions.q: Who took over from Russell T Davies as head writer/showrunner from 2010 to 2017?

source.notes: Moffat wrote 'Blink,' 'The Empty Child,' and 'The Doctor Dances.'

questions.q: The 1996 TV movie Doctor Who starred which actor as the Eighth Doctor?

source.notes: McGann's Doctor later appeared in 'The Night of the Doctor' minisode.

questions.q: Which 1970s actor, widely considered among the most iconic Doctors, wore a multi-colored scarf?

source.notes: Tom Baker's Fourth Doctor served 1974-1981, the longest run.

questions.q: Each Time Lord gets how many regenerations under classic series mythology?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: clarified 12 regenerations vs 13 incarnations to avoid '13 lives' ambiguity. Limit was later extended in-story.
